stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 30 smartphone models. The sample mean is 140.4 hours with a sample standard deviation of 39.4 hours. However, 3 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 27 observations have a mean of 130.6 hours and standard deviation of 24.5 hours. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 140.4, 95% CI = [125.7, 155.1]. Without outliers: mean = 130.6, 95% CI = [121.2, 140.0]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [121.2, 155.1]. Point estimate: 135.5 hours.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [121.2, 155.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
